title: Treasury Officer
description:
Job Title: Treasury Officer
Location: Singapore
Department: Group Treasury Centre
Reports To: General Manager / Group Treasurer

About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and responsible Treasury Officer to join our Treasury Centre team. The role will focus on the administration and maintenance of bank account/broker account records, signatory information, user access, and electronic banking tokens across multiple group entities.

Key Responsibilities

1. Bank Account Management

  • Maintain an up-to-date master list of all bank accounts within the Group (approximately 60 accounts), including account details, currency, and status.
  • Manage and update authorized signatories, account mandates, and approval limits in coordination with group companies and banks.
  • Administer internet banking user access, including the setup, modification, and removal of initiators and approvers.
  • Track and control the issuance and return of physical and digital bank tokens, ensuring security and compliance.
  • Liaise with banks for account opening, closure, and mandate updates.
  • Prepare regular account reports and ensure accuracy and consistency across all records.
  • Support internal and external audit requests relating to bank accounts and authorization.
  • Assist the Treasury team in maintaining compliance with internal policies and banking requirements.

2. Banking & Payment Operations

  • Execute and validate domestic and cross-border payments in a timely manner
  • Maintain bank account documentation
  • Coordinate with banks to resolve operational issues and maintain smooth workflows

3. Internal Lending Support

  • Assist with intercompany loan disbursements and repayments according to group policies.
  • Track loan balances and interest charges, supporting treasury schedules.

4. Reporting & Compliance

  • Assist with month-end closing and reconciliation of treasury-related accounts
  • Ensure compliance with MAS regulations, internal audit requirements, and group policies

Requirements

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
  • 2–4 years of relevant experience in treasury operations, bank account administration, or corporate banking preferred.
  • Strong attention to detail and good organizational skills.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel.
  • Good communication skills in English (Chinese proficiency is a strong plus for communicating with Headquarters and counterparts in Greater China).
  • Responsible, reliable, and able to work independently with minimal supervision.

postedCompany.description:
Zijin Mining Group Co. Ltd. (hereinafter referred to as “Zijin” or “the Group”) is a multi-national mining company headquartered in Mainland China and listed on both the Shanghai Stock Exchange (stock ticker number 601899) and Hong Kong Stock Exchange (stock ticker number 2899).

The Group is principally engaged in the prospecting, exploration, and mining of gold, copper, and other mineral resources with operations in 12 countries.

Today, Zijin is one of the largest gold, copper and zinc producers in China. Additionally, it generates the best efficacy, controls the largest volume of metal resources reserve, has the largest production volumes and the most competitive performance in the Chinese mining industry. In the Top 500 Enterprises of China released in 2020, Zijin'sefficiency indicator ranked 1st among non-ferrous (gold) mining enterprises. Under the list of Forbes Global 2000, it also ranked 3rd among global listed gold corporations and 7th among global non-ferrous metal corporations.

Zijin (Singapore) International Mining Pte Ltd is the overseas distribution center of Zijin Group. Its main business activities are trade in minerals, metal ores, non-ferrous metals, gold and black metals.
postedCompany.name: ZIJIN (SINGAPORE) INTERNATIONAL MINING PTE. LTD.
